sections.heading: Surgery
sections.content: Hepatic resection and liver transplantation are potentially curative treatment options for patients with early-stage HCC who meet specific criteria. They are the preferred treatments for patients who meet resection and/or transplant criteria [HCC-4, MS-41].

cards.name: Partial Hepatectomy
cards.condition: Potentially resectable HCC (CTP A/B, solitary mass, no major vascular invasion, adequate FLR)
cards.contraindications: Portal hypertension, inadequate FLR, major vascular invasion, multifocal disease (relative, except limited selected cases)
cards.detail: Approach: Open, laparoscopic, or robotic by experienced surgeons. Involves removal of the tumor-bearing portion of the liver. Indications: Potentially resectable HCC in patients with CTP A liver function (or highly selected CTP B without portal hypertension), solitary mass without major vascular invasion, and adequate future liver remnant [HCC-F]. Key complications: Liver failure (especially in cirrhosis), bleeding, bile leak, infection. Postoperative clinical decompensation risk increases with preoperative portal hypertension [MS-14]. Operative mortality: Low (≤5%) in well-selected patients at experienced centers [MS-14].

cards.name: Liver Transplantation
cards.condition: UNOS criteria (Milan) or downstaged, CTP B/C cirrhosis
cards.contraindications: Extrahepatic disease, macrovascular invasion, tumors beyond UNOS criteria not downstaged, inadequate organ availability
cards.detail: Approach: Cadaveric or living donor orthotopic liver transplantation. Indications: Patients meeting UNOS criteria (single tumor ≥2 cm ≤5 cm, or 2-3 tumors ≥1 cm ≤3 cm; AFP ≤1000 ng/mL; no macrovascular invasion; no extrahepatic disease) or downstaged to within criteria. Treats both the tumor and underlying cirrhosis. Preferred for patients with CTP B or C cirrhosis who are not surgical candidates [HCC-4, HCC-F]. Key complications: Surgical complications, risks of chronic immunosuppression, infection, renal dysfunction, malignancy. Pre-transplant mortality while waiting on list [MS-18]. Operative mortality: Variable; cited as a risk but specific rate not provided in source.
